title: Available Payment Instrument
description: An instrument type a handler accepts. What it accepts is declared along three distinct axes, each with its own mechanism: `constraints` (a bounded JSON Schema over the instrument's own fields), `accepts` (menus of derived/selected options), and `credentials` (per-credential-type requirements, discriminated by a data lookup).

constraints.description: A Constraint (bounded JSON Schema) over the instrument's OWN submitted fields: `required` (presence) and `properties.<field>.enum` (allowed values). This is the only part that is a runnable schema over the instrument.

accepts.description: Menus of derived/selected attributes the handler supports (e.g. `{ "brand": ["visa","mastercard"] }`). Consumers read these to OFFER options; the attribute is derived (e.g. card network from the PAN), not a submitted field, so it is a menu rather than a field-value constraint. A uniform map keyed by attribute, open to new attributes.

credentials.description: Accepted credential families with per-family requirements — each a Typed Constraint keyed by `type`. Discriminated by data lookup on the submitted credential's `type`; unknown types are handler/extension branches. A list, not a schema `oneOf`.
